text.title: Solo Deploy Checklist — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every solo deploy must pass. These are HARD rules — schedule pressure or feature excitement cannot override them.

rule.statement: PRE-DEPLOY: A fresh, restorable DB snapshot exists with a timestamp ≤ 30 minutes old AND a successful test-restore was demonstrated within the last 30 days; without both, the deploy is no-go.

rationale.source: Backup-without-restore-is-not-a-backup industry maxim + Patrick McKenzie writings on solo operations

rule.rationale: Untested backups fail at recovery roughly half the time; the demonstrated-restore window catches the silent rot.

rule.statement: PRE-DEPLOY: A rollback procedure MUST be documented in plain text (file, runbook, README) and reachable on a phone — not behind a VPN-only wiki — because the rollback case is the case where the laptop might be the broken part.

rationale.source: On-call accessibility design (Google SRE Book Ch. 11)

rule.rationale: Solo founders deploy from cafés and trains; a rollback gated on the laptop is no rollback at all.

rule.statement: PRE-DEPLOY: Deploys with breaking DB migrations MUST NOT begin within 4 hours of a hard meeting, end of workday, or boarding a flight; calendar conflict = abort.

rationale.source: On-call hand-off failure analysis (Beyer et al., SRE Book Ch. 14)

rule.rationale: Migration deploys produce their worst symptoms 30–90 minutes in; finishing them in flight or asleep is the most expensive option.

rule.statement: DURING-DEPLOY: A smoke-test suite (≥ one end-to-end flow that exercises auth + DB write + payment-or-equivalent) MUST run within 5 minutes of deploy completion; a failed smoke test triggers automatic rollback before any "stable" declaration.

rationale.source: Canary + smoke-test deploy practice (Humble and Farley, Continuous Delivery)

rule.rationale: Visual inspection of the home page is not a smoke test; the explicit end-to-end exercise catches the silent breakages that page-load misses.

rule.statement: POST-DEPLOY: A 30-minute observation window with active log + error-rate watch is mandatory before the founder context-switches to anything else; "stable" is declared only after the window with zero new error-class spikes.
rule.rationale: Most production-affecting issues surface in the first 30 minutes; leaving the chair before then puts the user-facing error budget at maximum risk.

example.title: Anchored deploy run
example.code: release: v0.42.0 pre: backup_snapshot_timestamp: 2026-05-20T13:14:00Z restore_demonstrated_within_30d: true rollback_runbook_reachable_on_phone: true no_calendar_conflict_4h: true during: deploy_started: 2026-05-20T13:32:00Z smoke_test_passed_at: 2026-05-20T13:35:40Z post: observation_window_min: 30 error_class_spike: none changelog_updated: true status: stable
example.note: Every gate ticked, observation honored, "stable" earned not declared.

example.title: Un-anchored deploy
example.code: "Pushed the migration before bed Friday. Will check Saturday."
example.note: No backup verification, no smoke test, no observation, no rollback within reach. Every rule violated; the next failure compounds with sleep deprivation.
